fills.note: Parallel to ruth_pilot_BCD_v0_3; the canonical Jonah BCD ref is the reviewer's call.

fills.value_json: {"_note":"MM Section 1 states explicitly: no scene-level or moment-level shifts; the whole prayer holds RELIGIOUS_WORSHIP throughout (recounting, confessing, vowing all inside worship).","scene_level":null,"moment_level":null}
fills.note: The one narrative word 'and he said' was cut at the verse seam and is not a narrating presence per the map, so no NARRATIVE framing override.
The approved arc_element list is entirely Ruth-shaped; a thanksgiving-psalm arc has no fits except AFFLICTION (the distress), so the movement is carried by declared mints in prose order.

vocabulary_additions.justification: Map 2.1/2.4: 'the rescue stated first' — the prayer tells its whole story in the first breath, I called/he answered, before walking the road.
Map 2.1: 'hurled into the deep, the seas closing' — the casting into the heart of the seas.
Map 2.1/Scene 2: 'banished from God's eyes yet turning toward his temple' — the prayer's hinge.
Map 2.1: 'down past the weeds, down to the roots of the mountains, the earth's bars shut behind' — the touched bottom.
Map 2.1: 'and then the lift, you brought my life up from the pit' — the single reversing movement.
Map 2.1/Scene 4: 'the speaker will sacrifice with a voice of thanksgiving and pay what he vowed.'
Map 2.1/2.4: 'The last word is the book's thesis... salvation belongs to YHWH.'

fills.note: PRIOR_PERICOPE_CARRY_FORWARD/PRIOR_ACTION_CONTEXT = the prayer J02 announced and the sea-hurling that preceded it; INSTITUTIONAL = temple-as-hearing-place and the vow/sacrifice cult; DIVINE = hesed and the presence Jonah fled; AUDIENCE = the reader knows the fish though the poem never names it.

RISING for the waters rising line by line; SLOWED as each notch goes deeper; STILLED for the calm temple-arrival; DECLARATIVE for the firm, bright close and thesis. Thanksgiving riding over remembered terror is carried by the WEIGHTED/STILLED pairing.
SLOWED (each line one notch slower), NARROWS (deepening toward the bottom), SETTLES (the quiet remembering and calm close).
ADVANCES = the turn of the whole story; REACTIVATES = answers J01's flight-from-YHWH question; PLANTS = hands the book its thesis; CLOSES = closes the prayer outward and forward.
Jonah is 'the one praying' — no role_in_scene_being value covers a worshiper at prayer.

vocabulary_additions.justification: Scene 1 role prose: 'the one praying — he called from his distress and was answered.' No approved role covers a praying suppliant.

fills.note: MM explicitly flags: 'the praying I — the poem never names him.' A register-critical marked unnaming.
Reuse: YHWH is the one called to, who answered and heard.
MM flags the mid-verse turn from 'he answered' (third person) to 'you heard' (second person).
No approved scene_kind fits a thanksgiving headline; this scene states the rescue before recounting the road.

vocabulary_additions.justification: Scene 1 title/3F: 'States the rescue before the road: the answered call is the headline.'

fills.note: Jonah remains the praying suppliant, here cast into the deep and turning toward the temple.

vocabulary_additions.justification: Scene 2 role prose: 'the one cast into the deep; the banished one who turns toward the temple' — still the praying suppliant.

fills.note: No marked naming flagged for Jonah in Scene 2.
Reuse: YHWH does the casting, owns the breakers and the temple.
The 'addressed throughout / yours' note is a relationship remark, not a marked naming the map calls attention to; left unmarked to avoid over-marking.
No approved scene_kind covers the recounting of distress with the temple-turn hinge.

vocabulary_additions.justification: Scene 2 recounts the casting into the deep and banishment answered by a face turned to the temple.

fills.note: Jonah at his lowest point, then lifted — still the praying suppliant.

vocabulary_additions.justification: Scene 3 role prose: 'the drowning man — engulfed, wrapped, sunk to the roots; then lifted, remembering, heard.'

fills.note: No marked naming flagged for Jonah in Scene 3.
Reuse: YHWH brings the life up from the pit, is remembered, receives the prayer.
MM flags 'YHWH my God — the runaway's first my of the book' as a marked reference.
No approved scene_kind covers the bottom-and-lift deliverance; this is the prayer's reversal.

vocabulary_additions.justification: Scene 3 title/3F: 'Touches bottom so the lift can be measured... you brought my life up from the pit.'

fills.note: In Scene 4 Jonah's function shifts to vowing — sacrifice with thanksgiving and payment of the vow.

vocabulary_additions.justification: Scene 4 role prose: 'the one vowing — sacrifice with thanksgiving, payment of what he vowed.'

fills.note: MM flags 'but I — set against the idol-keepers' as the marked contrastive reference.
Reuse: YHWH is owed the sacrifice and vow, and owns salvation.
No marked naming flagged for YHWH in Scene 4.
B8 functions as the contrast group who forsake their hesed; no approved role covers them.

vocabulary_additions.justification: Scene 4 role prose: 'the contrast — the ones who forsake their hesed' (those who hold to vain idols).

fills.note: 'unnamed others' is a relationship note, not a naming the map calls attention to; left unmarked.
Reuse of the approved VOW_SCENE — the vow with thanksgiving and the thesis.
The answered call out of distress — an appeal recounted within thanksgiving.
The whole prayer is recounted speech; speech_act marks the recounting voice.
Parallel restatement of P1: cried from Sheol's belly, YHWH heard.
Paired with P1 as the parallel cry/answer couplet.
YHWH credited with the casting; no approved proposition_kind covers it.

vocabulary_additions.justification: P3: 'You cast me into the deep, into the heart of the seas' — the act attributed to YHWH.

fills.note: The flood surrounding and the breakers/waves passing over.

vocabulary_additions.justification: P4: 'the flood was all around me; all your breakers and your waves passed over me' — no approved kind covers engulfment.

fills.note: waters_owner marks the 'your breakers/your waves' attribution to YHWH.
The casting (P3) caused the engulfing.
Jonah's inner speech 'I am driven away' — reuse SPOKE.
The banishment statement reads as a lament observation, stated without protest per the significant absence.
The determination 'yet I will look again toward your temple' — the hinge resolve.

vocabulary_additions.justification: P6: 'yet I will look again toward your holy temple' — a forward resolve, distinct from a vow or a plain speech act.

fills.note: Paired with P11 per FIG_0202 Temple-Turn-Pair (resolve to look / prayer's arrival).
The deepest engulfment — waters to the throat, the deep, weeds at the head (FIG_0203).

vocabulary_additions.justification: P7: 'The waters closed around me up to the throat; the deep surrounded me; weeds were wrapped around my head' — engulfment again, declared per axis-fill rule.

fills.note: Down to the roots of the mountains, the earth's bars behind him forever (FIG_0198 lowest point).

vocabulary_additions.justification: P8: 'I went down to the roots of the mountains; the earth — her bars were behind me forever' — the descent's lowest point.

fills.note: 'forever' is the speaker's word for how final the bars felt, per the MM, not a time-frame.
The single reversing line — YHWH brought his life up from the pit.

vocabulary_additions.justification: P9: 'And you brought my life up from the pit, YHWH my God' — the lift, no approved kind covers it.

fills.note: At the faint, Jonah remembered YHWH — the honest center.

vocabulary_additions.justification: P10: 'As my soul fainted within me, I remembered YHWH' — remembering, distinct from PERCEIVED.

fills.note: Reuse: the prayer came to YHWH, to the holy temple.
caused_by the remembering (P10); paired_with P6 per FIG_0202 Temple-Turn-Pair.
Idol-keepers forsake their hesed — the contrast.

vocabulary_additions.justification: P12: 'Those who hold to vain idols forsake their hesed' — no approved kind covers forsaking.

fills.note: Reuse: the vow to sacrifice with thanksgiving and pay what was vowed.
contrast_with_proposition encodes the 'but I' set against the idol-keepers (P12).
Reuse: declares the thesis, salvation belongs to YHWH.
Terminal proposition of the pericope; no outgoing link.

No further decomposition needed. The propositions are already at clean single/parallel-event granularity; engulfment lines (P4, P7) and the call/cry couplet (P1, P2) are kept as map-granularity events rather than split, since each already carries one speech act and a tight clause set.

remarks: Genre group is POETIC_SUNG (PRAYER), so the pericope register stays RELIGIOUS_WORSHIP and the map explicitly disclaims any scene/moment register shift — register_overrides null on both levels. The approved L2 lists are heavily Ruth-shaped; a thanksgiving psalm from the deep required several declared mints on arc_element, scene_kind, role_in_scene_being, and proposition_kind (all justified from the map's own prose). YHWH is mapped to DIVINE_AGENT throughout and Jonah to a minted SUPPLIANT (VOWER in the vow scene). The whole pericope is recounted speech, so each proposition carries a speech_act marking the recounting voice (STATES_AS_TRUE, with STATES_LAMENT_OBSERVATION at P5 and VOWS at P13). FIG_0202's temple-turn pair is encoded as paired_with between P6 and P11. book_context_ref guessed as jonah_pilot_BCD_v0_3 — reviewer to confirm the canonical Jonah BCD ref.
